Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0xC000 "ALIENRESCUE-C000" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Alien Rescue (J1).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
This game has a BASIC program to display the title screen, so the following steps were taken to retain this: 1. When the title screen was shown, the BASIC program from $0801 to $0B20 was dumped to disk. This is because the first part of the program gets overwritten later with a SYS 8192 ($2000) statement to start the game once it's finished loading. 2. When the breakpoint at $2000 was hit, the BASIC program dumped in step 1 was restored into memory before the game memory dump was taken. 3. The SYS 2818 statement on line 300 has also been changed to SYS 8192 ($2000), which is actually the games entry point after loading. This allows the code to jump to the games entry point after the title screen is displayed. 4. A new routine was placed at $C000 which sets up the correct VICII settings and necessary BASIC pointers and runs the title screen program.
Also, this game had a form of simple copy protection which checked that the first letter of the game filename 'R' (as part of 'RESCUE') was present at $0342 in the tape buffer, just as it would be if the game was loaded from tape and would hang if not. Therefore, the BNE $2014 instruction at $2014 was changed to BNE $2016 to remove this check.
Note: to dump, simply set a breakpoint on $2000
